decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly dump powerful creatures, such as Griselbrand and Atraxa, into the graveyard and then bring them back into play using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead. The deck also includes disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize to protect the combo. The use of efficient mana acceleration cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual allows for explosive plays early in the game. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats and powerful abilities while disrupting their game plan.

The main idea behind this Legacy Dimir Tempo deck is to maintain early pressure on the opponent by quickly deploying threats while disrupting their game plan with a variety of disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Daze, and Force of Will. The deck aims to control the board and keep the opponent off balance with removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out, while also drawing cards and setting up its own game plan with Brainstorm and Ponder. Moonshadow and Nethergoyf provide strong threats that can close out the game quickly if left unchecked.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble the Doomsday combo with The Fantasticar as a key piece. The deck aims to quickly generate mana with cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual, and dig for combo pieces with Brainstorm, Ponder, and Consider. Thassa's Oracle serves as the win condition once Doomsday is resolved. The deck also includes disruption cards like Stifle, Daze, and Force of Will to protect the combo. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and Predict helps to set up the combo.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain control of the game by combining disruptive elements like Force of Will and Daze with efficient threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Cori-Steel Cutter. The deck aims to disrupt opponents' game plans while maintaining pressure with aggressive creatures and burn spells like Lightning Bolt. The underlying strategy is to utilize tempo plays like bouncing opposing threats with Boomerang Basics and Into the Flood Maw while deploying threats of its own to win the game quickly. The mana base is optimized for consistency and disruption with a focus on blue and red sources.

The main concept of this deck is to control the tempo of the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Daze, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. The deck aims to maximize card advantage through cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm, as well as using Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student for additional card draw. The mana denial strategy with Wasteland and disruption spells like Force of Will help maintain board control and keep the opponent on the back foot.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying threats like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler while disrupting the opponent's plans with counterspells like Daze and Force of Will. It also aims to control the game with cards like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base and Lightning Bolt to remove key threats. The goal is to apply pressure early and maintain tempo advantage by keeping the opponent on the back foot while also drawing cards and manipulating the deck with Ponder and Brainstorm.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive cards like Stifle and Wasteland. It also includes card advantage engines like Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to outvalue opponents. The deck utilizes a mix of white and blue cards to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, it includes some unique cards like Quantum Riddler and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian to provide additional utility and card selection. This archetype excels at outmaneuvering opponents and grinding out incremental advantages.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game with counterspells, removal, and card draw while eventually winning with powerful threats like Teferi, Time Raveler, The One Ring, and Murktide Regent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares, while also generating value and card advantage through cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes utility lands like Wasteland and Mystic Sanctuary.

This deck focuses on utilizing lands as control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to lock down the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale while generating value through cards like Exploration and Life from the Loam. The deck also has a combo finish with Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token. It can protect its game plan with cards like Karakas and Boseiju, Who Endures, making it difficult for the opponent to overcome the control elements of the deck.

The main focus of this Mardu Aggro deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize, while applying pressure with creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also utilizes cards like Guide of Souls and Orcish Bowmasters to control the board and protect key creatures. With a mix of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruption, along with a solid mana base featuring lands like Arid Mesa and Karakas, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The main concept of the deck is to use a combination of fast mana sources like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal along with control elements like Force of Will and Daze to protect powerful combo pieces like The Fantasticar and Murktide Regent. The deck also utilizes card selection and manipulation tools like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Mishra's Bauble to sculpt the perfect hand and draw into combo pieces quickly. By efficiently utilizing resources and controlling the board, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces and win the game quickly and decisively.

The core strategy of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's plays using cards like Force of Will, Stifle, and Daze while generating card advantage with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd provides protection and life gain against aggressive decks, while Teferi, Time Raveler offers control options and tempo advantage. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Consign to Memory to handle threats. The land base includes utility lands like Karakas and Mystic Sanctuary to support the deck's control game plan.

The deck focuses on cheating high-cost creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand into play quickly using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also utilizes cards like Omniscience and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to generate more value from playing big creatures. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats and quick mana acceleration, while also having counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its game plan. It is a high-risk, high-reward combo deck that aims to win the game in explosive fashion.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the Living End combo to quickly win the game. By cycling through cards like Street Wraith and Striped Riverwinder, the player can fill their graveyard with creatures and then cast Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield. Cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation help protect the combo from disruption, while cards like Shardless Agent and Generous Ent provide additional value and card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and win the game in a single explosive turn.

Utilize fast mana acceleration and graveyard recursion to cheat powerful creatures into play, disrupting opponents with hand disruption spells and controlling the board with life gain and removal spells. The key strategy is to quickly reanimate big threats like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty to overwhelm the opponent and win the game before they can establish their own game plan.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to aggressively control the board through efficient removal and disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck aims to quickly ramp up creatures with Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and pump them up with Anthem effects like Voice of Victory. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Erode provides additional reach and disruption, making it a versatile and high-pressure deck that can overwhelm opponents quickly. The land base is optimized for consistency and mana efficiency to keep the pressure up throughout the game.
